sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Manchester United have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, displaying resilience with late goals. West Ham United, struggling in 18th place, have managed 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses in their recent 5 games, with defensive frailties evident as they've failed to keep a clean sheet in 10 matches.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, results are split with each team securing a win (Manchester United 2-1, West Ham 0-2). West Ham have often scored first in these matchups, but United's home advantage at Old Trafford could play a decisive role.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Manchester United, sitting 9th with 21 points, boast a decent attack (21 goals, 6th in the league) but struggle defensively (20 goals conceded). West Ham's poor season (15 goals scored, 27 conceded) reflects their 18th position, with low possession and frequent defensive lapses.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Bruno Fernandes remains United's creative hub with 5 assists (top in the league) despite underperforming his xG. For West Ham, Jarrod Bowen's 3 goals could pose a threat, though missing key player Lucas Paquetá may hinder their midfield dynamism.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: United's ability to create big chances (33, 7th in the league) and dominate ground duels (51.6% won) could exploit West Ham's leaky defense. West Ham's tendency to concede first (9/10 games) and struggle with possession (often below 40%) might see them rely on counter-attacks.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Sofascore's prediction (56% home win) and United's superior form and home advantage, they are favored to win. West Ham's defensive issues and lower league standing suggest they will struggle to contain United's attack, though they might nick a goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Old Trafford between Manchester United and West Ham United ended goalless with a scoreline of 0-0. Manchester United dominated possession and showed greater intent in the final third, frequently entering the opposition's penalty area. However, they failed to convert their opportunities into goals. West Ham, on the other hand, were more conservative but managed to create a notable chance, though they couldn't capitalize on it. Both teams displayed discipline defensively, with minimal bookings and no major disciplinary issues.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Manchester United started the game with strong attacking intent, maintaining consistent pressure during the opening minutes. Their momentum waned briefly as West Ham found their footing and pushed forward with a series of attacking spells around the midway point of the half. The visitors showed dangerous moments during this phase, creating sustained pressure. However, Manchester United regained control towards the latter stages of the half, producing several waves of attacking play and looking more likely to break the deadlock before the whistle. West Ham had a late surge, but it wasn't enough to shift the balance significantly.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For Manchester United, Bruno Fernandes stood out with his creativity in midfield, frequently attempting to unlock the West Ham defense with key passes. Amad Diallo also impressed with his involvement in the final third, showing composure and vision. Joshua Zirkzee had opportunities to score but needs to be more clinical. On the West Ham side, Aaron Wan-Bissaka was exceptional defensively, making crucial interventions and contributing to build-up play. Jarrod Bowen looked threatening on the counter but missed a big chance to give his side the lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Manchester United's approach relied heavily on controlling possession and pushing high up the pitch, which allowed them to dominate territory. Their frequent entries into the final third and penalty area suggest they could exploit gaps if they sharpen their finishing. West Ham adopted a more compact shape, focusing on quick transitions to catch United off guard. Their aerial dominance was evident, and they may look to capitalize on set pieces in the second half. Both teams will likely need tactical adjustments to break the deadlock, with United possibly needing more incisiveness and West Ham requiring better finishing.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half is poised to be an intriguing battle as Manchester United will likely continue to push for a breakthrough with their territorial advantage at home. Their attacking intent in the closing stages of the first half suggests they could find the net if they maintain that pressure. West Ham, while more defensive, have shown they can be dangerous on the break and will aim to exploit any overcommitment from United. Fatigue could play a role, and substitutions might be key in deciding the outcome of this match.
